text.title: Fine-tuning Datasets — Preparation, Training, Deployment Core Rules

rule.statement: Near-duplicate detection MUST use MinHash (or SimHash) at ≤0.85 Jaccard; never exact-match only.
rule.rationale: Exact-match misses paraphrase duplicates which inflate apparent quality.

rule.statement: Held-out test split MUST be carved BEFORE any cleaning or augmentation, and version-locked.
rule.rationale: Cleaning after split risks leakage; locked split enables comparisons across runs.

rule.statement: All examples MUST be in one target format (Alpaca OR ShareGPT OR OpenAI JSONL); never mix.
rule.rationale: Mixed-format datasets confuse the loss curve and break framework loaders.

rule.statement: PII (emails, phone, names where applicable) MUST be stripped or pseudonymised before training.
rule.rationale: Memorisation of PII creates compliance risk.

rule.statement: Post-train evaluation MUST run LM-Evaluation-Harness or equivalent across general benchmarks (MMLU, HellaSwag, ARC) — not only the niche task.
rule.rationale: Catastrophic forgetting is invisible without general benchmarks.

rule.statement: Pre-deploy smoke suite MUST run ≥20 representative prompts and pass before promotion.
rule.rationale: Many finetunes degrade only on specific phrasings; smoke catches it.
